headline: Child Marriage Is Legal in Malaysia. In Multiple Ways.
context: Suhakam reported 1,856 child marriages between 2020 and 2025 across civil and syariah jurisdictions, with state laws setting minimum ages between 14 and 16.

cards.big: Federal law says 18. State law says 14. Both are valid.
cards.sub: The legal minimum depends on which jurisdiction, which religion, and which state you live in.

cards.big: Syariah courts approved 94% of child marriage applications in 2024.
cards.sub: The 'judicial safeguard' is a rubber stamp. Refusal rates are statistically invisible.

cards.big: 13 state legislatures must independently amend their Islamic family laws.
cards.sub: Six states have not tabled amendments. Kelantan and Terengganu have explicitly refused.

cards.big: 73% of married girls drop out of school within two years.
cards.sub: Child marriage is an education crisis, an economic crisis, and a health crisis simultaneously.

cards.big: If 13 jurisdictions must agree to protect a child, is the child actually protected?
cards.sub: Federalism is not a reason. It is the mechanism of inaction.

cards.big: A right that depends on your postcode is not a right. It is a lottery.
